title: Orleans boating guide: what is new in 2026
html:
New Orleans offers a fresh perspective on boating in 2026, with the Mississippi River and Lake Pontchartrain providing excellent departure points for a day afloat. Prices start from around EUR 90 per person for a shared tour, making it accessible for most visitors. BoatHire24 connects you with local operators for a hassle-free booking experience.

Where to set off in Orleans

Several real marinas and launch points give you access to the region's waterways. Orleans Marina, Seabrook Harbor, and South Shore Harbor are popular for boat rentals. For a scenic trip, head to Bayou St. John or the Rigolets pass. Nearby towns like Mandeville and Slidell offer additional options for day trips across Lake Pontchartrain.

Which boat to rent

  1. Pontoon boat ideal for family cruising, holds up to 12 guests, from EUR 250 for half-day.
  2. Speedboat perfect for water sports and quick trips, capacity 6 guests, from EUR 180 per hour.
  3. Small motorboat a versatile choice for fishing or exploring, up to 4 guests, from EUR 120 for 2 hours.
  4. Sailing yacht for a relaxed day on the lake, sleeps 6, from EUR 300 for full-day charter.
  5. Luxury yacht like the Maiora 26m, for up to 12 guests with crew, from EUR 1,500 per day.

Check availability on yacht charter Orleans to compare options and find the right vessel for your group.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Orleans?

Rental prices in Orleans range from EUR 90 per person for shared tours to over EUR 2,000 for a full-day crewed yacht. Here is a breakdown by type:

  • Shared bayou tour: from EUR 90 per person (2-hour trip)
  • Small motorboat half-day: from EUR 120 (4 hours, no licence needed)
  • Catamaran full-day: from EUR 500 (up to 8 guests)
  • Crewed yacht full-day: from EUR 1,200 (including captain and fuel, up to 12 guests)

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Orleans?

No licence is required for most small motorboats under a certain power limit, but larger boats may need proof of experience. Local operators will confirm exact requirements when you book. For specific models like the Limpopo Axopar, see the Limpopo listing for details on power and licence needs.

For the best weather in Orleans, book between March and May or October and November, when the heat is mild and afternoon thunderstorms are less frequent. The prevailing southerly wind keeps the lake conditions pleasant.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 90, EUR 2,000 per trip
  • Peak months: April to June, September to October
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Capacity: 4 to 12 guests
  • Currency: Euro (EUR)

Questions about boat rental in Orleans

Can I rent a boat in Orleans without a licence?

Yes, many rentals do not require a licence for boats under 15 feet or with small engines, but always confirm with the operator.

What is the best time of year to rent a boat in Orleans?

Spring and autumn offer the most comfortable conditions, with fewer storms and lower humidity than summer.

How far can I travel from Orleans in a rental boat?

From Orleans Marina, a 20-nautical-mile trip across Lake Pontchartrain takes you to Mandeville in roughly one hour at cruising speed.

Are there any age restrictions for renting a boat?

Most operators require the renter to be at least 25 years old, though some accept 21 with a clean driving record.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Orleans and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
